transition_record.writer_rule: Only the Memory Transition Gate database role may write protected memory state.
transition_record.atomicity: Protected memory state, version, indexes or projections owned by the transition, and audit evidence commit in one PostgreSQL transaction or all roll back.
transition_record.replay_key: transition_request_id is unique within immutable scope and memory_record_id
transition_record.concurrency: A successful transition consumes exactly the expected state version and creates exactly one monotone new state version.

invariants.assertion: tenant_id, area_id, and applicable project_id or session_id never change across versions.
Every memory record and derived version retains immutable provenance and source references.
Protected memory tables cannot be changed by general application, consumer, model, or inference-adapter roles.
An unavailable or failed audit append rolls back the protected memory transition.
An inactive, under-assessment, rejected, or quarantined candidate is excluded from normal retrieval.
Promotion creates a new immutable governed memory version and does not rewrite the candidate or its provenance.
Retrieval returns freshness and provenance, and never represents stale, expired, or unknown-freshness content as current fact.
Deletion or anonymization reaches governed derivations, indexes, caches, and retrieval projections, subject only to an evaluated legal hold.
Queries and writes are constrained by authenticated tenant_id and area_id before content evaluation.
Startup and restore remain not-ready until incomplete transitions, deletion cascades, projections, and audit continuity are reconciled.
Unknown state, scope, provenance, freshness, classification, authorization, or commit outcome blocks mutation and current-fact retrieval.

crash_rule: Before commit, the transition and audit roll back. After commit, recovery uses the authoritative memory ledger. An unknown commit outcome is reconciled by transition_request_id and is not blindly retried.
tenant_root_boundary: Unresolved by this contract; no root-scope exception or sentinel value is authorized.
